scope: Free genealogy platforms for cousin-tree discovery and bulk GEDCOM export

platforms.name: FamilySearch.org
platforms.operator: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints / Intellectual Reserve Inc.
platforms.model: Single shared collaborative tree (globally merged, ~1.4 billion indexed profiles)

cousin_tree_discovery.mechanism: Navigate to shared ancestors via the global tree; cousins who have contributed those ancestors are visible. The 'Possible Duplicates' indicator shows overlapping contributions.
cousin_tree_discovery.notes: Linked Trees feature (2022+) allows Ancestry trees to be linked to FamilySearch persons — if a cousin linked their Ancestry tree, their data is partially visible on FamilySearch

gedcom_export.scope: Own contributed profiles only — no bulk export of arbitrary subtrees or cousins' data
gedcom_export.format: GEDCOM 5.5 for personal export; API returns GEDCOM X (JSON) format
gedcom_export.notes: No mechanism to bulk-download cousins' data even if it exists in the tree

api.auth: OAuth 2.0 — register app at https://www.familysearch.org/developers/ for appKey
api.rate_limiting: Higher limits with authenticated app key; unauthenticated requests heavily restricted

hunters_surnames.Spence_Beaumont_TX: Likely present via TX census/vital records indexing
hunters_surnames.Boehme_Lavaca_Co_TX: Present via LDS German-Texas indexing programs
hunters_surnames.Lepik_Brown_Co_KS: Sparse — unusual Estonian/Czech surname, smaller county
hunters_surnames.Mattingly_KY: Heavily indexed — major KY Catholic family, excellent coverage

limitations: No bulk GEDCOM export of cousins' data
API returns GEDCOM X format requiring conversion to GEDCOM 5.5
Data quality variable due to community editing and incorrect merges
Bot protection (Incapsula/Imperva WAF) blocks automated curl requests to main site
Hints system (Ancestry record matches) not accessible programmatically

platforms.name: WikiTree.com
platforms.model: Single collaborative tree (~40M+ profiles, wiki-style with named profile managers and Trusted Lists)

cousin_tree_discovery.mechanism: GEDCOMpare tool: upload your GEDCOM, WikiTree shows existing profiles matching your ancestors. If a cousin created a profile for a shared ancestor, GEDCOMpare surfaces it as a match.
cousin_tree_discovery.notes: The most direct free mechanism to discover what cousins have already entered for shared ancestors

upload.notes: Trees >5000 must be split. Upload at https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Special:UploadGEDCOM

gedcompare.description: After GEDCOM upload, shows comparison table of your people vs existing WikiTree profiles. Confirm/reject suggested matches. Reveals which ancestors cousins have already entered.

api.auth: None required for public profiles; session cookie for private profile access and higher rate limits
actions: getProfile — profile by WikiTree ID or PageId
getPerson — profile by WikiTree ID or User ID
getBio — biography text (wiki or HTML format)
getPeople — multiple profiles by key or relationship
getRelatives — parents, children, siblings, spouses in one call
searchPerson — search by first+last name, location, dates
getWatchlist — profiles on logged-in user's watchlist
getConnectedProfilesByDNATest — profiles linked via DNA test
api.rate_limiting: Anonymous callers heavily rate-limited (requires both first AND last name for searchPerson; frequent requests result in 'Limit exceeded'). Authenticated callers have higher limits.

database_dumps.access: SFTP — requires WikiTree membership and email request to jamie@wikitree.com
files: dump_people_users — all public people (WikiTree ID, names, birth/death dates/locations, privacy-controlled)
database_dumps.license: Non-commercial preferred; individual permissions granted by WikiTree

wikitree_plus.description: Independent free service by Ales Trtnik. SQL-like search against WikiTree dump data. Ancestor/descendant reports, surname search by location. No code required.

Spence.status: Profiles exist — Spence-1 confirmed public (Margaret Spence, 19th century)
Spence.name_study: Category:Spence Name Study exists as stub — no content yet

Mattingly.status: Multiple profiles expected — well-represented KY Catholic family
Mattingly.name_study: Category:Mattingly Name Study exists as stub — no content yet

Boehme.status: Sparse — German immigrant surname, less covered on WikiTree

Lepik.status: Very sparse — Estonian/Central European immigrant, rare in US genealogy databases

Baity.status: Present — colonial-era NC Quaker/Baptist records well-represented

limitations: GEDCOM upload limit of 5,000 people (split required for larger trees)
Anonymous API access heavily rate-limited for searchPerson
Data quality depends on community contributors — variable
GEDCOMpare requires manual profile-by-profile confirmation (can be tedious for large trees)
Database dump access requires individual permission grant from WikiTree

platforms.name: Geni.com

cost.pro_tier: Geni Pro (paid, ~$59/year) — full World Family Tree access and full GEDCOM export

platforms.model: Single global 'World Family Tree' (~300M+ profiles) with smart-matching merges across users

cousin_tree_discovery.mechanism: Upload GEDCOM; Geni attempts to match profiles against global tree. Relationship paths to public profiles visible in free tier.
cousin_tree_discovery.notes: Full lateral cousin navigation requires Geni Pro

import.confirmed_via: Page JS: G.GEDCOM_import_enabled = true; G.GEDCOM_import_start = true

export.free_tier: Own profiles only — not the global tree or cousin data
export.pro_required: Full GEDCOM export of connected tree requires Geni Pro

api.auth: OAuth access token required — no anonymous access
api.confirmed: Unauthenticated test returned: 'You have a missing or invalid access token. This call can only be made with an access token.'
api.notes: API documentation sparse and potentially outdated as of 2026

limitations: GEDCOM export of cousin/global tree data requires paid Pro subscription
API requires OAuth — no anonymous access
Smart-matching merges can create confusing incorrect data combinations
Development appears stagnant since MyHeritage acquisition
Free tier severely limited for genealogical research beyond immediate family

platforms.name: MyHeritage Family Tree Builder
platforms.cost: Application free; online matching features require MyHeritage subscription
platforms.primary_use: Multi-source GEDCOM merge and management tool

gedcom.export: Yes — GEDCOM 5.5 from any local tree
gedcom.merge: Yes — 'Merge Trees' function for combining multiple GEDCOMs locally

features: Import GEDCOM from Ancestry, WikiTree, FamilySearch exports
Record matching against 39.3 billion MyHeritage records (subscription required to view matches)
No individual limit on local tree size
limitations: Desktop app is a funnel toward MyHeritage subscription
Smart Matches (cousin data from other MyHeritage members' trees) require paid Data subscription (~$149/year)
MyHeritage has history of changing free-tier limits without notice
Windows and macOS only — no Linux version
platforms.recommended_workflow: Best used as the GEDCOM merging tool: import Ancestry GEDCOM + WikiTree export + FamilySearch GEDCOM X converted export, use Merge Trees to deduplicate, export clean merged GEDCOM

platforms.name: Find a Grave
platforms.cost: Free to search and view; free to add memorials
platforms.primary_use: Death/burial verification, headstone photos, family connections on memorials

cousin_tree_discovery.possible: Indirectly — family links on memorials show relatives
cousin_tree_discovery.notes: Not a substitute for tree data; useful for confirming dates and burial locations

api.notes: No public API. Ancestry's acquisition means data is not openly exportable.

hunters_surnames_expected_coverage.Spence_Beaumont_TX: Good — Jefferson County TX cemeteries (Forest Lawn, Magnolia, Beaumont Municipal)
hunters_surnames_expected_coverage.Henslee_Dallas_Beaumont_TX: Good — Dallas County and Jefferson County TX
hunters_surnames_expected_coverage.Boehme_Lavaca_Co_TX: Good — German-Texas cemeteries (Zion Lutheran, St. John's) well-indexed
hunters_surnames_expected_coverage.Lepik_Brown_Co_KS: Sparse — small Kansas county, patchy coverage
hunters_surnames_expected_coverage.Mattingly_KY: Excellent — KY cemeteries among most thoroughly indexed on Find a Grave; Marion/Nelson County KY
hunters_surnames_expected_coverage.Baity_NC: Good — Surry/Forsyth County NC Piedmont area

platforms.practical_notes: Use as manual verification tool — confirm dates and burial locations before adding to GEDCOM. Search at https://www.findagrave.com/search/memorial/search

platforms.name: BillionGraves
platforms.primary_use: GPS-indexed cemetery records; secondary check when Find a Grave lacks a record

api.notes: Exists but not publicly documented; Cloudflare protection blocks automated access (confirmed via live test)

platforms.hunters_surnames_notes: Particularly useful for German-heritage Texas cemeteries (Boehme/Lavaca Co) and Kansas frontier cemeteries (Lepik/Brown Co) where LDS indexing focused

open_source_projects.name: Gramps
key_features: FamilySearch plugin for two-way sync with FamilySearch Family Tree
Python API for programmatic data manipulation
Integration with Gramps Web for collaborative access

gramps_web.description: Self-hosted web app + REST API backend. Can run on Hunter's VPS (152.53.169.245). Collaborative, privacy-first, GEDCOM import/export, AI chat assistant integrated.

open_source_projects.name: webtrees
key_features: Multi-user collaborative — invite cousins to contribute
Interactive family tree charts, maps, reports
open_source_projects.recommended_use: Deploy on Hunter's VPS as canonical merged tree and family collaboration portal

open_source_projects.name: GEDCOM X
open_source_projects.description: FamilySearch's open successor to GEDCOM 5.5. Defines JSON and XML serialization. FamilySearch API returns GEDCOM X format. Requires conversion to GEDCOM 5.5 for use with most desktop tools.

workflow.name: Maximum genealogical value without scraping Ancestry

steps.title: Export from Ancestry (one-time, legitimate)
steps.action: Ancestry > Tree > Export Tree > Download as GEDCOM
steps.notes: Hunter's own data, legally owned. No ToS issue. This becomes master input for all downstream platforms.

steps.title: Upload GEDCOM to WikiTree — run GEDCOMpare
steps.action: Upload at https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Special:UploadGEDCOM (split if >5000 people). Run GEDCOMpare to see which ancestors already have WikiTree profiles (created by cousins or other researchers).
steps.output: List of WikiTree IDs matching Hunter's ancestors. Use API (getProfile, getBio, getAncestors) to fetch full data for each match. Export merged GEDCOM from WikiTree.

steps.title: Cross-reference on FamilySearch
steps.action: Create free account. Search key ancestors by name + location. Navigate single tree to find cousin-contributed data.
steps.output: FamilySearch Person IDs for matching ancestors. Use API (/platform/tree/persons/{pid}/ancestors) to pull ancestor chains in GEDCOM X format. Convert GEDCOM X to GEDCOM 5.5.

steps.title: Verify deaths/burials on Find a Grave
steps.action: Search each ancestor at https://www.findagrave.com. Focus: Jefferson Co TX (Spence/Henslee), Lavaca Co TX (Boehme), Marion/Nelson Co KY (Mattingly), Surry/Forsyth Co NC (Baity), Brown Co KS (Lepik).
steps.output: Confirmed burial dates, locations, headstone photos as additional sources.

steps.title: Merge all sources in MyHeritage FTB or Gramps
steps.action: Import all GEDCOMs (original Ancestry, WikiTree export, converted FamilySearch export) into FTB (quick) or Gramps (power). Use Merge Trees / duplicate detection. Export merged GEDCOM.
steps.output: Single authoritative GEDCOM incorporating data from all free platforms.

steps.title: (Optional) Deploy webtrees on VPS for family collaboration
steps.action: Install webtrees on 152.53.169.245. Import merged GEDCOM. Share URL with cousins for browsing, source contribution, and GEDCOM download.
steps.output: Ongoing collaborative family history portal that continues to grow.

what_this_gets_that_ancestry_alone_canno: WikiTree profiles with primary source citations and biographies from cousins' research
FamilySearch linked records — census, vital, military records often from different collections than Ancestry
Find a Grave cemetery data — headstone photos and burial locations confirming or correcting tree data
Complete data ownership — everything in Gramps/webtrees is yours, exportable, no subscription lock-in
